data:
After months of general operation without a specific name or website, LVMLabs is finally official and formed!
(For those who don’t know, it was coordinated and done through real life w/ friends or through chats)
“LVMLabs”, pronounced “L V M Labs” or “Lima-Victor-Mike Labs”, got its name from the founder Ryan (or me). It carries the suffix of my US FCC amateur radio callsign, and then has “Labs” to signify we do quite a lot of things here, just like a real lab.
Some cool new changes:
data.design--assets#Design \u0026amp; Assets: For now, our logo. Our logo is just a basic bold “LVM” and a regular “Labs”. It’s availble in both italics and non italic. Nothing really special to see, but oh well. Logo’s a logo. We also have multiple sized favicons, in PNGs, ICOs, and SVGs, so it works for everyone. (looking at you iPhone users!)
data.goals-for-the-future#Goals for the Futur:
Some goals include:
Dedicated domain (probably lvmlabs.org) Changing of all other existing works to have LVMLabs branding (branding? is that the word?) 501(c)(3) status with HCB (w/ The Hack Foundation, d.b.a. Hack Club) 73!
data.so-what-will-we-do-now#So\u0026hellip; w: Not much will change. Our previous projects and efforts (listed on the homepage for new people) will still continue, just in a more professional and organized (yay, long and cool words, very professional) way.
data.website#Website: After a few hours of tinkering around with HTML, I called it quits and decided to use Hugo w/ Hextra. It’s a cool static site generator with a bunch of themes, and I decided Hextra looked good. Right now, it’s a bit empty, considering we don’t have any official media or content nor official blog posts / documentation. As time progresses and we collect more documentation professionally logged (if this is considered professional), it will come up here.

data.3262026-2-am---everything-done#3/26/2026:
The Journal is a direct export from Hack Club Stasis and may have formatting issues. Time is in UTC.
3/26/2026 2 AM - Everything Done!Time spent: 0.01666666666666667h
1 min logged as I need to log some time for the journal.
Everything has been completed, as per the Submission Guidelines.
Checklist
Originality - yes, this is my project. Shipped and complete: CAD assembly with all electronics and parts - Yes Firmware present - yes (mobiflight) Sanity checked - yes 3D Render - “render”-ish, but yes. BOM in CSV - Generic and Stasis, yes. PCB source files - Gerber, yes. Any other files - Mobiflight config files, etc, yes. STEP files for CAD files, yes. (cad/) Readable and organized into folders, yes. Readme:
Description - yes Sentences on why - yes Pictures - plenty, yes Screenshot with full model - yes PCB screenshot - yes Wiring diagrams - yes (schematic) BoM in table format - yes
data.7-segments#7 Segments: Uses MAX7219, 6 digits each. Data cascaded into U11 from U13 DOUT, only U13 recieves DIN from Pico/MobiFlight. All data is ran through Logic Level Shifter (Shifter pins used in place of MAX7219 pins)
data.a320-radio-management-panel#A320 Radio M: Made for MSFS2020, MobiFlight, RP2040, and the FlyByWire A32NX
data.a320rmp-wouldnt-be-possible-without#A320: VATSIM, https://vatsim.net FlyByWire Simulations, https://flybywiresim.com Oakland vARTCC, https://oakartcc.org Hack Club, https://hackclub.com Stasis, https://stasis.hackclub,com
data.and-most-importantly#And most importantl: @witherman3000, https://witherman3000.com Thanks for looking through my project! 5NN THX FER RPRT DE KO6LVM SK CL
data.bom-1#BoM: Stasis BoM populated with LCSC and Amazon parts JLCPCB will be added after quote
data.buttons-1#Buttons: Name IC Pin SW(N) L(N) i.e. S2 L2
data.buttons-2#Buttons: Due to pin limit we are using a multiplexer. Multiplexer: TI CD74HC4067 https://www.lcsc.com/product-detail/C1545936.html SW# Button - To L# on Multiplexer E# - Grounded S# on Multiplexer - GPIO # on Pico COMMON INPUT/OUTPUT Pin 1 on Multiplexer - GPIO 4 (6)
data.data#Data: Name IC Pin Pico GP S0 10 GP0 S1 11 GP1 S2 14 GP2 S3 13 GP3 C I/O 1 GP4
data.dimensions#Dimensions: 155.67mm * 95.3mm (5mm wall) 7seg height: 9mm Pi Pico USB-C Clone w/ Headers: 4mm Female Headers: 4mm “Standoff” case height: 8.5mm Heat set insert OD: 5mm Heat set insert 3d print hole: 4.6mm All other components dimensions in their Datasheets i.e. In this case, construction dual-point rect at 129.413x69.83mm and then a center-point-rect at its footprint

data.knob-1#Knob: Knob - made in Onshape, from encoder specs from LCSC
data.lcsc#LCSC:
C = Capacitor R = Resistor L = LED B = Button IC = IC/Chip Note: Any item’s quantity that’s unreasonably high (i.e. 100 capacitors) is due to the minimum purchase count in LCSC. However, these are so cheap that 100 vs 4 won’t really make a difference, as it is the matter of cents.
Part LCSC P# Qty Cost (Per) Total 0.1uF C0603 C1591 100 $0.0028 $0.28 10uF C0603 C96446 20 $0.0176 $0.35 6x6 B C49234152 20 $0.0306 $0.58 6d7s L C47471 3 $1.7325 $5.20 220 R C22962 100 $0.0013 $0.13 22K R C2907015 100 $0.001 $0.11 Rot Enc C470754 3 $2.08 $6.23 Green L C19273151 100 $0.006 $0.60 Log Lvl IC C155176 2 $0.3292 $0.66 7seg CT IC C6705351 4 $1.5703 $6.28 Multiplexer IC C6525 2 $0.7064 $1.41 SPST C1788492 2 $0.6153 $1.23 Header Pin F C7499336 4 $0.24
data.leds#LEDs: Name Pico GP Purpose U3 GP14 VHF1 U4 GP15 VHF2 U5 GP16 VHF3 U6 GP17 HF1 U7 GP18 HF2 U8 GP19 SEL U9 GP20 AM
data.lid-1#Lid: 1mm OD circles for LEDs 16mm vert and horz spacing for buttons and LEDs Everything else done to PCB spec 5mm border
data.logic-level-shifter#Logic Level Shifter: A input, Y output 3.3V logic to 5V logic Pins Used: VCC - to 5V VBAT GND - to GND #1OE - to GND to enable 1 #2OE - to GND to enable 2 #3OE - to GND to enable 3 1A - to Pico GP21 (34) for CLOCK 1Y - to Both 7219 “CLK” (13) 2A - to Pico GP22 (32) for LOAD 2Y - to Both 7219 “LOAD” (CS#) (12) 3A - to Pico GP27_A2 (29) for DATA 3Y - to U13 ONLY “DIN” (1)
data.max7219#MAX7219: Takes 5V Takes the following inputs: Clock i. Shared, “metronome” Data i. Actual data, goes into U13 “DIN” only ii. Pico -> Logic Shift -> U13 DIN -> U13 DOUT -> U11 DIN Load i. Shared, command to refresh or “commit”
data.new-parts#New Parts: Logic Level Shifter - 3.3V to 5V - TI SN74AHCT125N - https://www.lcsc.com/product-detail/C354152.html For MAX7219 MAX7219 will pull VBUS 5V
data.other-parts#Other Parts: Looking at TI CD74HC4067PWR Switch to TI CD4067BM96, LCSC C6525 Do this in schematic/pcb.
data.parts#Parts: 2x 6-digit 7-segment display - ZHIHAO FJ3661AH - https://www.lcsc.com/product-detail/C47471.html 13x 4-pin Button - hanxia HX-6x6x7.3-TP FT - https://www.lcsc.com/product-detail/C49234152.html 2x MAS7219 - https://www.lcsc.com/product-detail/C492111.html 7x Green 0603 LED - YONGYUTAI YLED0603G - https://www.lcsc.com/product-detail/C19273151.html - LEDs only for top two rows of buttons
data.pcb#PCB: DRC final run Silkscreen final Copper pour final rebuild Sanity checked
data.pcb-1#PCB: 2-layer design now 1mm power traces (yes, I don’t need this much but it’s fine) Made it 2-layer for cheaper JLCPCB costs
data.pcb-2#PCB: Copper Pour for GND GND nets on Pico Component Data Routing
data.pcb-3#PCB: MAX7129 routing completed 0.1uF and 10uF capacitors added in parallel from VBUS to GND next to the multiplexer Main power traces (0.7mm) done Main power vias (0.7mm OD 0.4mm ID) done Basic naming and silkscreening done
data.pcb-4#PCB: 1x MAX7219 routed to 6dig7seg Logic Level Shifter 1/2 outputs complete Multiplexer inputs complete Basic silkscreens Capacitor Placement Pi Placement
data.pcb-5#PCB: Initial Layout Routing Required Routing done for: Some small resistors and capacitors Routing semi-done for: Buttons and Multiplexer; must move back-mounted resistors back onto the front Rest must be started / done. 4-Layer EasyEDA Pro
data.readme#README: WIP - on local disk. Will be uploaded ~26-27th.
data.reference#Reference: For the MAX7129 pins use the logic level shifter in pins instead
data.rotary-encoder#Rotary Encoder: ALPSALPINE EC11E15244B2 https://www.lcsc.com/product-image/C470754.html Has push button, but is not used at the moment, but can be mapped. SW13 / Left A - GP5 / P7 (A) B - GP6 / P9 (B) C - GND (Encoder GND) D - GND (Button GND) E - GP7 / P10 (Button) 6 - GND 7 - GND SW14 / Right A - GP8 / P11 (A) B - GP9 / P12 (B) C - GND (Encoder GND) D - GND (Button GND) E - GP10 / P14 (Button) 6 - GND 7 - GND
data.schematic-1#Schematic: Completed All ICs now SMD (no THT) Not fully sanity-checked 2x MAX7219s, 1x Multiplexer, 1x Logic Level Shifter, 2x 6-digit 7-segment displays, 13x buttons, 7x LEDs, 1x SPST switch, 2x rotary encoders
data.spst-switch#SPST Switch: YUEN FUNG MT-0-102-A101-M200-RS https://www.lcsc.com/product-detail/C1788492.html SPST, 125V Max, 5A Max THT
data.stats--info---a320-rmp#Stats / Info - A3: Airbus A320 Radio Management Panel (Upper) https://docs.flybywiresim.com/pilots-corner/a32nx/a32nx-briefing/flight-deck/pedestal/rmp/ 13 Buttons (12 Main + 1 A-B Switcher) Mains: VHF1/2/3, HF1/2, AM, NAV, VOR, ILS, MLS, ADF, BFO ii. A-B Switch (Backlit) 1 SPST Switch 2 Rotary Encoders (Stacked) Two 6-digit 8-segment displays One “VHF 2/3 Selected” LED 12 LEDs Occompanying Main buttons
data.sw13-left-mhz#SW13 (Left, MHz): Name Pico GP Purpose A GP5 Left B GP6 Right E GP7 Button
data.sw14-right-khz#SW14 (Right, KHz): Name Pico GP Purpose A GP8 Left B GP9 Right E GP10 Button
data.switch#Switch: As mentioned before, SPST GP11 / P15
data.to-do#TO DO: Wire Pi to other Components Sanity-check with others (schematic and PCB) Ground net, pour, or traces
data.u11-led2-and-u13-led3#U11-LED2 and U13-L:
UA in Name states Both U11 and U13
Name Pico GP Purpose U13 DIN GP27_A2 Data In UA LOAD GP22 Load All UA CLOCK GP21 Clock
data.what-is-this#What is this?: The A320RMP is a small desktop radio management panel for use with Microsoft Flight Simulator and MobiFlight, and is based off the Raspberry Pi RP2040 platform.
data.why-was-this-made#Why was this made?: While flying on VATSIM with MSFS2020, I realized it was very difficult to switch frequencies through the mouse and scroll wheel in the FlyByWire A32NX. So, here is my version of the A320 RMP.

data.features#Features: 3 Dedicated MX Switches for PTT and Deafen Toggles 4 Dedicated On-Off Switches for RX, SPKR, XC, and XCA Toggles in CRC (INOP of 4/2026 due to CRC AFV Restrictions) 3 Green, Blue, and Red LEDs for PTT and Deafen Custom Board with RP2040 Custom KMK Firmware for ARM Cortex-M0+ on CircuitPython
